title: JavaScript Errors and Exceptions Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of try...catch, throw, and common error types in JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct syntax for a simple try...catch block?
Does the 'finally' block execute even if an error occurs and is caught?
options: No, only if no error occurs.
No, only if an error is NOT caught.

questions.question: What is the result of calling a variable that does not exist in the current scope?
How do you throw a custom error object in JavaScript?
What happens when you call a string as if it were a function (e.g., "str"())?
